note: Updated quarterly by admin. Contact CHFA, DOH, or county housing offices to verify availability before advising clients.

CHFA-HTF.name: CHFA Housing Trust Fund
CHFA-HTF.description: CHFA HTF provides gap financing for affordable multifamily projects. Funds are competitive statewide.

CHFA-CCLA.name: CHFA Construction/Permanent Loan Advantage
CHFA-CCLA.description: Below-market first mortgage financing for affordable housing. Available year-round with rolling applications.

DOLA-HTF.name: Colorado DOLA Housing Trust Fund
DOLA-HTF.description: State housing trust fund administered by DOLA. Rural areas receive priority weighting.

CDBG-DR.name: CDBG Disaster Recovery (DOLA)
CDBG-DR.description: Not currently available. Previous round closed. Watch for new HUD allocations.
CDBG-DR.warning: No active round — funds exhausted from 2024 disaster declaration.

Denver-AHTF.name: Denver Affordable Housing Trust Fund
Denver-AHTF.description: City of Denver AHTF — largest local fund in state. Strong preference for deep affordability (30% AMI).

Boulder-HTF.name: Boulder County Housing Trust Fund
Boulder-HTF.description: Boulder County HTF — strong local funding base. Priority: workforce and deep affordability.

HOME-CO.name: HOME Investment Partnerships (State)
HOME-CO.description: Federal HOME funds administered by DOLA. Typically layered with LIHTC. Annual allocations.

DOH-AHTF.name: DOH Affordable Housing Trust Fund
DOH-AHTF.description: Colorado Division of Housing (DOH) trust fund for gap financing. Supports new construction, acquisition/rehab, and preservation. DOH administers separately from CHFA — different application cycle.

DOH-HDG.name: DOH Housing Development Grant
DOH-HDG.description: DOH development grants for predevelopment, acquisition, and infrastructure related to affordable housing. Can be layered with LIHTC and other state sources.

DOH-HHPG.name: DOH Homeless Housing Program Grant
DOH-HHPG.description: DOH grants for permanent supportive housing and homeless prevention projects. Requires service partnership with local continuum of care.

NHTF-CO.name: National Housing Trust Fund (Colorado)
NHTF-CO.description: Federal National Housing Trust Fund allocated to Colorado, administered by CHFA. Exclusively targets extremely low income households (30% AMI). Strong layering source with 9% LIHTC.

PROP123-AHTF.name: Proposition 123 Affordable Housing Fund
PROP123-AHTF.description: State funds from Proposition 123 (2022 ballot measure) dedicating TABOR surplus to affordable housing. Administered by DOLA. Jurisdictions must opt in. Supports land banking, gap financing, and rental assistance. First full-year allocation cycle.
PROP123-AHTF.note: New program — first substantive awards expected Q3 2026. Participating jurisdictions listed in prop123_jurisdictions.json.

PROP123-LBTF.name: Prop 123 Land Banking Trust Fund
PROP123-LBTF.description: Dedicated Prop 123 subprogram for land acquisition and banking for future affordable housing development. Can reduce TDC in future LIHTC applications by removing land cost.

PAB-CO.name: Private Activity Bond Volume Cap (Colorado)
PAB-CO.description: Colorado's annual Private Activity Bond volume cap. Required for 4% LIHTC deals — project must use at least 50% PAB financing to qualify for automatic 4% credits. Cap is allocated by CHFA on first-come basis with quarterly review. When cap is exhausted, 4% deals cannot proceed until next calendar year.
PAB-CO.warning: 63% of 2026 cap already committed. Projects targeting Q4 closing should apply immediately.

OZ-EQUITY.name: Opportunity Zone Equity Investment
OZ-EQUITY.description: Qualified Opportunity Zone (QOZ) capital gains deferral incentive under IRC §1400Z-2. Projects in designated OZ census tracts can attract equity from investors seeking capital gains tax deferral. Can be layered with LIHTC but complex structuring required. Not a direct funding program — availability depends on investor market.
OZ-EQUITY.note: OZ designation expires 12/31/2028. Remaining deferral benefit diminishes each year. Projects must be in designated OZ tracts (see QCT/DDA overlay).

NMTC-CO.name: New Markets Tax Credits (Colorado CDEs)
NMTC-CO.description: Federal NMTC program provides 39% tax credit over 7 years for investments in low-income communities. Colorado CDEs include Colorado Growth and Revitalization Fund and Colorado Enterprise Fund. Can be combined with LIHTC but dual-credit structures are complex and require specialized legal counsel.
NMTC-CO.note: NMTC + LIHTC dual structures are rare but possible for mixed-use developments with commercial components.

TIF-LOCAL.name: Tax Increment Financing (Local)
TIF-LOCAL.description: Municipal TIF districts can provide gap financing for affordable housing through property tax increment capture. Availability varies by municipality — Denver, Aurora, Colorado Springs, Lakewood, and Fort Collins have active URA/TIF programs. Requires municipal approval and urban renewal authority designation.
TIF-LOCAL.note: Check with local urban renewal authority for active TIF districts. Not all municipalities have enabling legislation.

pabVolumeCap.note: Colorado 2026 PAB volume cap. Updated quarterly from CHFA bond allocation reports. Projects must apply for PAB allocation before requesting 4% LIHTC determination.
